summary: Traces a 2D image (TOP) into line strips on the GPU. Replaces the 2D-input mode of the Polygonize POP, which is now 3D-only. Optimised for converting silhouettes, edges, and contours into line geometry.

key_params.name: top
key_params.note: Source TOP to trace — typically a thresholded or edge-detected image

key_params.name: threshold
key_params.note: Brightness threshold that separates traced regions from background

key_params.name: step
key_params.note: Sampling step — larger values yield fewer, coarser points

key_params.name: closed
key_params.note: Force closed line strips (auto-connect last point to first)

key_params.name: smooth
key_params.note: Number of smoothing passes applied to the traced contour

common_gotchas: Output is a POP of line strips, not a TOP — feed it into a Render Simple TOP, Render TOP, or Triangulate POP downstream.
Migration: if you previously fed a 2D TOP into a Polygonize POP, switch to Trace POP — Polygonize POP no longer accepts 2D input.
Very high-resolution inputs can produce huge line counts. Pre-resize the source TOP to keep tracing cheap.
